item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A short, yet interesting and somewhat wholesome piece.

One of many great examples that shows you don't need to horrify the audience to make a successful scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such a sweet little anomaly. It's cute, I like it very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet, maybe too much so, but it's too pleasant to not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also in a major city, and they can't relocate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's Euclid because of its ability to access Foundation documents and data, which might present an information breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the SCP +1. But shouldn't it be safe not Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. It's not a pseudo-682 and it's not a 999. I'd agree with the Foundation being a little out of character in this article however. Drinking tea that appeared through anomalous means with out testing is probably not the best idea… Other than that, it doesn't seem to have too much wrong with it. (Though, that's coming from me and I know I'm not exactly reliable for this.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is terribly sweet and I love it. +1.

(But can I make two small suggestions?)
– hide block

1. The wording of the second footnote bugs me a little. Might I suggest changing it to "A 'suitable container' is any clean wares intended to store liquid for human consumption."?

2. Both footnotes should end with periods, since they're otherwise complete sentences.

I reiterate, though, this is a lovely wholesome article and I'm glad I got to read it today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's so cute! The 'morale usage' aspect reminds me of series one, in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love the nice tea ghost

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generation of tea will always occur, but certain variables (Such as flavor and brand of the tea)

"such" shouldn't be capitalized.

and… other than that, I don't actually see any. Oh well — maybe the one early on primed me, who knows. In any event, on re-reading it I like it much more. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Grammatical errors?

*cocks shotgun*

Where are they?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is a great idea, but the grammatical errors are distracting and I don't understand what's up with Lab 4. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of don't get it. Do they get messages for giving this thing tea, or do they get tea themselves? Does it just give them more tea? And wall adjacent to what, exactly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On my first read-through I thought the anomaly was duplicating or modifying the tea types taped to the wall. I think the difficulty is that both the collected, dried tea leaves and the potable solution produced by steeping said leaves can both be referred to as "tea". Perhaps bumping up the science jargon in this regard would be appropriate? YMMV

I wonder about the tea in the pot - does it stay hot until drunk? Does it remain hot when removed from the classroom, ie. is the hot tea itself anomalous?

And the entity that makes it all happen, would she mark/edit a paper left behind with a nice bag of tea? What would it take for her to give a "F - SEE ME AFTER CLASS" kind of response?

I think you can push the premise of this SCP, and look forward to seeing you do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I clicked on that link I was sure it was going to be the spy kitten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good idea, but wouldn't the Foundation consider this to be at least partially dangerous? I mean, the ghost could communicate and manifest things, and seems to have access to vital information. This could blossom into an information breach as far as they're concerned.

Cute skip, though. Still, I suggest you revise it a bit. Semi-enthusiastic +1 from me.
And no, I didn't read Roget or LordMetalton's comment before I commented, didn't realize how similar it would come to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. The anomaly showed that it can learn info from the foundation from those that enter it's area of effect. Given that it's also capable of communicating this information, I think they should have considered this a potential secrecy risk. Cute, seemingly harmless things have turned out to be hostile in the past.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given that she went above and beyond in cleaning, it's likely that she just thinks it's too good for her and that she's incredibly surprised someone would give her something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, what does the tea ghost have against pu-erh? Rude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The cuteness aspect of the article fell kind of flat for me, it seems to just be general platitudes and a vaguely helpful note. In addition, I found the behavior of the Foundation here to be out-of-character, I can't imagine why they would let the researchers drink potentially anomalous tea or keep one of the notes. -1

Congrats on being the first article in the new series posted outside of the contest, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took me two reads to realize how it worked. Maybe the fact that it's 4 in the morning was a factor to that…

Anyways, I can't not upvote the cute tea ghost. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cute as all hell, upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tentative +1 because while I was a little confused as to what was going on, I really like Tea Ghost and short simple articles…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An interesting concept, but the character of the room isn't really coming through for me, and the table doesn't seem to explore the effect in an interesting manner. One or the other could sway my vote, but it's a sure -1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright. Here's to series four, I guess. Thanks to everyone who critted (no really like you guys are like 12/10 even though i can hardly remember names right now i'm shaking so bad). I hope y'all go off and write some great stuff for S4 :)

(Picture taken by me.)

Beautiful wonderful coole fanart
